Day - Constellation Complex 19
gopala Iyyer's method. The third ignores the addition of one to
the remainder for arriving at the birth star.
The day begins with Sun-rise in all these reckonings. It
will be found that on any day the interval between two times
is sixty three Vighatis. This is a· little over twenty minutes. To
reduce this interval, it is better to take the nakshatra from
one table and the weekday from another. This is only one
possible method.
The figures are in Ghatis and Vighatis. The time of Sun
rise given in many Panchangas appears to be the Indian
Standard Time. This has to be converted into the local mean
time. Using two tables together, if necessary, the time of birth
can be corrected within three minutes only.
